KO_H01: 윗자리에 있는 사람들이나 권력을 가진 기관이 자기들의 권위를 이용해 어느 한쪽으로 치우치지 않는 공정한 기준과 질서를 만들어 주지 않는 상황에서는, 사람들에게 억지로 시키지 않아도 각자가 스스로 다른 사람도 자기와 같은 권리를 가진 존재라고 인정하고 그 권리를 해치지 않으려고 행동하는 일이 결코 쉽지 않다.
KO_H02: 혁명은 낡은 지배를 무너뜨리고 세상을 바꾸려는 일이지만, 실제로는 오랫동안 서로 적으로 싸워 온 상대가 혁명 뒤에 새로운 지배자가 되어 사람들 위에 군림하는 경우도 있다. 그러므로 혁명 이후에 그런 오랜 적이 새 주인이 되지 않는 것만으로도 이미 운이 좋은 일이라고 할 수 있다.
KO_H03: 자유가 있다는 말은 하고 싶은 대로 하기만 하면 된다는 뜻이 아니라, 스스로 선택하고 그 선택의 결과도 자기 책임으로 감당해야 한다는 뜻이다. 많은 사람들은 남이 정해 주지 않는 상태에서 스스로 결정하고 그 결과를 떠맡아야 하는 일을 부담스럽고 두렵게 느끼기 때문에 자유를 두려워한다.
KO_H04: 사람이 겪는 온갖 불행은 크게 보면 한 가지 사실에서 나온다. 그것은 사람이 혼자 자기 방에 조용히 머물면서 가만히 생각하고 견디지 못하고, 끊임없이 밖으로 나가 무언가를 하거나 다른 자극을 찾으려 한다는 것이다.
KO_H05: 겉으로 멀리 이동하는 여행보다 더 오래 걸리고 더 어려운 여행은 자기 마음속으로 들어가 자신이 누구인지 알아보는 일이다. 그리고 자기 자신을 피하지 않고 받아들이며 어떤 사람으로 살지 스스로 선택한 사람만이, 비로소 남에게 기대거나 휘둘리지 않고 진정한 의미로 다른 사람에게 가까이 다가갈 자격을 얻는다.
KO_L01: 나는 오늘 아침에 평소보다 이른 시간에 잠에서 깼다. 그리고 따뜻한 물로 얼굴을 씻은 뒤에 아침밥을 먹었다. 그다음 학교로 갔고, 하루 일과를 마친 뒤 저녁이 되었을 때 집으로 돌아왔다.
KO_L02: 그 사람은 어제 서울을 출발해서 기차를 타고 부산까지 갔다. 그리고 부산에 갔다가 하루가 지난 오늘 다시 원래 있던 쪽으로 돌아왔다.
KO_L03: 나는 어제 친구를 직접 만났다. 그리고 그 친구와 함께 카페로 가서 커피를 마셨다. 우리는 그곳에서 서로 이야기를 나누며 시간을 보냈다.
KO_L04: 주말에 나는 가족들과 함께 집에서 멀지 않은 공원으로 갔다. 우리는 그 공원에서 걸으며 산책을 했고, 그러다가 벤치에 앉아서 미리 준비한 도시락을 함께 먹었다.
KO_L05: 나는 아침에 지하철을 타고 회사로 출근했다. 점심시간에는 회사 근처 식당에 가서 밥을 먹었다. 그리고 오후가 되자 다시 회사 일로 돌아가 계속 일했다.
EN_H01: Among all the things people might think they should be afraid of in a hard national crisis, the most dangerous thing is fear itself. By "fear" I do not mean careful caution based on real facts. I mean a kind of panic that has no clear object, is not guided by reason, and is greater than the situation actually justifies. That kind of terror makes people freeze, lose confidence, and stop taking the strong, necessary actions that could change a situation from decline and withdrawal into recovery and progress.
EN_H02: A person who spends time fighting people or forces that are cruel, evil, or destructive must be very careful not to slowly become cruel, evil, or destructive in the same way. The warning is that, while trying to defeat what is monstrous, a person can start copying its methods, values, or lack of humanity. The image of staring for a long time into a deep abyss means giving long, intense attention to darkness, evil, hopelessness, or chaos. The idea that the abyss also gazes into you means that what you study or struggle against for too long can begin to affect your mind, character, and identity, so that it changes you from the inside.
EN_H03: When a person gets power over other people, that power usually makes the person more likely to act in morally bad ways, such as becoming selfish, arrogant, dishonest, or abusive. When a person has absolute power, meaning power with no real limits and no one able to stop or correct them, that harmful effect becomes complete and extreme. The statement that great men are almost always bad men means that people widely admired as great because they shape history often achieve that position through morally harmful behavior or are damaged by the power they hold. This can happen even when they do not officially rule others by legal authority, because simply having strong influence over what people think or do can also corrupt a person.
EN_H04: The phrase "the moral universe" means the long course of human history and human moral life. Saying that its arc is long means progress toward what is right usually takes a very long time and does not happen quickly. Saying that it bends toward justice means that, over time, history can move toward fairer treatment, better laws, and more right than wrong. But the added condition means this does not happen automatically or by some magic force. It happens only if enough real people choose to act, work, protest, sacrifice, and push society in that direction. In other words, justice wins only when enough human hands help bring it about.
EN_H05: Inside situations that are hard, painful, confusing, or failing, there is often some real chance to create something better. That is what it means to say opportunity lies in the middle of difficulty. But seeing that chance is not easy. A person must be brave enough to stop trusting a method, plan, or belief just because it once felt familiar or safe, especially when it has already been shown not to work. In other words, recognizing opportunity often requires giving up the comfort of clinging to failed certainty and accepting the risk of trying a different path.
EN_L01: On the morning of today, I woke up earlier than my usual or necessary waking time. After getting out of bed, I cleaned my teeth with a toothbrush and toothpaste. Then I put on the clothes I planned to wear for the day instead of staying in sleep clothes. After that, I ate my morning meal while sitting at the table in the kitchen of my home. When I finished breakfast, I left home, got into my own car, and drove myself to the place where I work.
EN_L02: She went on foot to a store located at the street corner, meaning she did not use a car, bus, or other vehicle to get there. At the store, she bought two items: one bottle of water and one sandwich. After making those purchases, she went to a park, sat down on a bench there, and ate the sandwich and drank the water as her lunch.
EN_L03: Yesterday, during the afternoon rather than the morning or late evening, my brother traveled to my house to spend time with me. While he was there, we sat on the couch together and watched a movie. Later, when it was time for dinner, we did not cook food ourselves; instead, we arranged for pizza to be delivered or prepared for us by ordering it. He stayed at my house until about nine o'clock at night, and then he left and went back to his own home.
EN_L04: This morning, I traveled to the library by riding a bus instead of using a private car or walking the whole way. While I was there, I gave back three books that I had previously borrowed and that had reached their required return date, meaning they needed to be returned by then. After returning those books, I borrowed two different books to take home. When I was finished at the library, I traveled back to my home, and I arrived there before lunchtime.
EN_L05: When my workday ended, I did not go straight home. Instead, I stopped at a grocery store on the way. There, I bought at least two specific food items, which were milk and eggs. After leaving the store, I drove to my home in a car. Once I was home, I prepared a dinner that was not elaborate or complicated. After eating, I turned on the television and watched a news program.
